title: Phase 4 Review: Returning, Using & Getting Around

meta.focus: Review of Kanji, Vocabulary, and Grammar from Lessons 1–2.

sections.title: Part 1: Conversation Check
sections.instructions: Read each conversation and select the best answer.

items.title: Conversation 1
items.context: Two coworkers talking at the office before one leaves for home.

lines.jp: 今日は 何時に 帰りますか。
七時に 帰ります。帰り道に 切手を 買います。
友だちに 本を 送りますか。
はい。友だちに 本を 送りたいです。大切な 友だちです。

items.question: What does B plan to do on the way home?
choices: Buy stamps and send a book to a friend
Cut fish at the station store
Work at the company until midnight
items.answer: Buy stamps and send a book to a friend
items.explanation: B says 帰り道に切手を買います (will buy stamps on the way home) and 友だちに本を送りたいです (wants to send a book to a friend).

items.title: Conversation 2
items.context: A teacher and student at school discussing how the student gets to school each day.

lines.jp: 毎日 何で 学校に 来ますか。
電車に 乗って 来ます。家は 学校から 少し 遠いです。
駅は 家から 近いですか。
はい、近いです。駅まで 歩いて、電車に 乗ります。

items.question: How does B get to school each day?
choices: Walks to the station and rides the train
Rides a bus all the way from home
Borrows a car from the teacher
Works near the school each morning
items.answer: Walks to the station and rides the train
items.explanation: B says 駅まで歩いて、電車に乗ります — walks to the station, then rides the train to school.

items.title: Conversation 3
items.context: Two close friends casually planning to visit a mountain on a day off.

lines.jp: 今日、山まで 電車に 乗って 行かない？
少し 遠いけど、電車で 行くよ。
分かった！着いて、山の 道を 歩こう。

items.question: What do they plan to do after arriving at the mountain?
choices: Send photos to friends at home
Work at a shop near the mountain
Ride the train back home immediately
items.explanation: B says 着いて、山の道を歩こう — after arriving, let's walk the mountain path.

sections.title: Part 2: Grammar Scramble
sections.instructions: Construct the sentence by clicking the words in the correct order.

items.q: Rikizo arrived at the station and bought stamps.
items.explanation: りきぞ + は (topic) + 駅 + に (destination of 着く) + 着いて (te-form, sequential action) + 切手 + を (object) + 買いました. に marks where Rikizo arrived; を marks what was bought. が is a subject marker, not a topic marker here.

items.q: Every day I wake up, put on a shirt, and go to the company.
items.explanation: 毎日 + 起きて (wake up, te-connector) + シャツ + を (object of 着る) + 着て (wear, te-connector) + 会社 + へ (direction) + 行きます. Time words like 毎日 can appear at the start or after the first verb. Use を for what you wear; へ marks direction for 行く.

items.q: Rikizo walked along the mountain path.
items.explanation: りきぞ + は (topic) + 山 + の (modification) + 道 + を (path traversed with 歩く) + 歩きました. The を particle with movement verbs like 歩く marks the path or space traversed, not just a direct object.

items.q: The station is close, so let's walk there.
items.explanation: 駅 + は (topic) + 近いです (predicate adjective) + から (because — reason connector after a predicate) + 歩きましょう (polite volitional: let's walk). から after an adjective+です expresses reason. が would mean 'but', creating a contradiction with walking being the natural choice.

items.q: I use the computer and read books.
items.explanation: パソコン + を (object) + 使って (use — te-form connector) + 本 + を (object) + 読みます. The te-form 使って links two sequential actions. Both パソコン and 本 are objects of their respective verbs and take を.

passage.jp: りきぞは 毎日 六時に 起きます。
passage.en: Rikizo wakes up at 6 every day.

passage.jp: 起きて、シャツを 着て、会社へ 行きます。
passage.en: He wakes up, puts on a shirt, and goes to work.

passage.jp: 電車に 乗って、三十分で 駅に 着きます。
passage.en: He rides the train and arrives at the station in thirty minutes.

passage.jp: 会社は 駅から 少し 遠いですが、毎日 歩いて 行きます。
passage.en: The company is a little far from the station, but he walks there every day.

passage.jp: 会社で パソコンを よく 使って、たくさん 働きます。
passage.en: At the company he often uses the computer and works hard.

passage.jp: 帰り道に 切手を 買って、友だちに 本を 送りたいです。
passage.en: On the way home he wants to buy stamps and send a book to a friend.

questions.q: りきぞは 毎日 何時に 起きますか。
questions.explanation: りきぞは毎日六時に起きます — Rikizo wakes up at 6 o'clock every day.

questions.q: 会社は 駅から どうですか。
choices: とても 大きいです
questions.explanation: 会社は駅から少し遠いですが — the company is a little far from the station.

questions.q: 帰り道に 何を したいですか。
questions.a: 切手を 買って、本を 送りたいです
choices: 切手を 買って、本を 送りたいです
友だちから お金を 借ります
シャツを 着て 帰ります
山の 道を 歩きます
questions.explanation: 帰り道に切手を買って、友だちに本を送りたいです — on the way home he wants to buy stamps and send a book to a friend.

sections.title: Part 4: Kanji Readings
sections.instructions: Select the correct reading for the [bracketed] word.

items.q: [帰り道]を 歩きました。
items.explanation: 帰り道 (the way home / route back) is read as kaerimichi. 帰 (N4.1) + 道 (N5.6).

items.q: 先生から ノートを [借りました]。
items.explanation: 借りる (to borrow) is read as kariru; past tense form karimashita. 借 (N4.1).

items.q: [切手]を 買いました。
items.explanation: 切手 (postage stamp) is read as kitte. 切 (N4.1) + 手 (N5.13).

items.q: 電車に [乗りました]。
items.explanation: 乗る (to ride / to board) is read as noru; past tense norimashita. 乗 (N4.2).

items.q: [歩いて] 学校へ 行きます。
items.explanation: 歩く (to walk) in te-form is aruite. 歩 (N4.2).

items.q: 駅は 家から [近い]ですよ。
items.explanation: 近い (near / close) is read as chikai. 近 (N4.2).

items.q: 友だちに 本を [送りました]。
items.explanation: 送る (to send) is read as okuru; past tense okurimashita. 送 (N4.1).

items.q: 毎日 六時に [起きます]。
items.explanation: 起きる (to wake up) in polite present form is okimasu. 起 (N4.1).

sections.title: Part 5: Context & Vocabulary
sections.instructions: Select the best word to complete the sentence.

items.q: 毎日 起きて、______ を 着て、学校へ 行きます。
items.explanation: シャツ (shirt) fits: every day I wake up, put on a shirt, and go to school. You wear (着る) a shirt — シャツを着る.

items.q: 駅は 家から 近いから、毎日 ______。
items.explanation: 歩きます (walks) fits: because the station is close, walking there every day is natural. 乗ります (ride) is less logical when something is close; 帰ります (go home) and 起きます (wake up) don't fit the context.

items.q: 先生に ノートを ______。
items.explanation: 借りました (borrowed) fits: I borrowed a notebook from the teacher. 先生にノートを借りる = borrow a notebook from the teacher. に marks the person you borrow from.

items.q: 遠足は 学校から 少し ______。電車に 乗って 行きます。
items.explanation: 遠いです (is far) fits: the excursion is a bit far from school, which is why they take the train. 近いです (close) would make a train journey unnecessary.

sections.title: Part 6: Grammar Check
sections.instructions: Choose the correct particle or verb form to complete the sentence.

items.q: 友だち ______ 本を 送りたいです。
items.explanation: Use に to mark the recipient of a sending or giving action. 友だちに本を送る = 'send a book to a friend'. を already marks 本 (the thing sent); the person receiving it takes に, not を.

items.q: 電車 ______ 乗って、学校へ 行きます。
items.explanation: The verb 乗る (to ride / board) always takes に to mark the vehicle boarded. 電車に乗る = 'ride the train'. Compare: 電車で行く (go by train) — で marks the means of transport with 行く, but に is required with 乗る specifically.

items.q: 今、パソコンを 使って、本を ______。
items.explanation: 読んでいます (te-form + います) expresses an action happening right now. 今 (now) signals a present progressive. 読みました is past (finished reading), 読みましょう is a suggestion (let's read), 読みません is negative (don't read).

items.q: 学校は 遠いです ______ 毎日 電車に 乗って 行きます。
items.explanation: から after a predicate (遠いですから) expresses reason or cause: 'because it is far'. けど means 'but', suggesting a contrast rather than a reason. まで means 'until / as far as'. より means 'more than' (for comparisons).
